# momentum-universe — LLM integration guide

This repository is a point-in-time momentum + swing research system for
Indian equities (NSE). It answers the PURELY TECHNICAL questions — what to
buy, how much, suggested stops, and rotation horizons — from ~13 years of
vendored data, credential-free. It deliberately models NO fundamentals,
shareholding, news, or valuations. That diligence layer is YOUR job as the
LLM: take the system's picks, research each ticker with your own tools
(fundamentals APIs, web search, filings), and confirm or veto.

## One-time machine setup

    git clone <repo-url> momentum-universe
    cd momentum-universe
    ./start.sh        # bootstraps .venv, installs deps, builds the local DuckDB
                      # (exit the interactive menu once it loads — setup is done)

## MCP server

Read-only stdio server exposing the allocation stack:

    .venv/bin/python -m fortress.mcp_server

The server resolves all paths from its own location — the client's working
directory does not matter.

### Claude Code

The repo ships a project-scoped `.mcp.json`; opening Claude Code inside the
repo detects the "momentum-universe" server automatically (approve when
prompted). To register it globally (usable from any directory):

    claude mcp add momentum-universe --scope user -- \
      /ABS/PATH/TO/momentum-universe/.venv/bin/python -m fortress.mcp_server

### Codex

Append to `~/.codex/config.toml`:

    [mcp_servers.momentum_universe]
    command = "/ABS/PATH/TO/momentum-universe/.venv/bin/python"
    args = ["-m", "fortress.mcp_server"]

### Any other MCP client (generic stdio config)

    command: /ABS/PATH/TO/momentum-universe/.venv/bin/python
    args:    ["-m", "fortress.mcp_server"]

## Tools

- swing_allocation_plan(capital=500000, hb_slots=3, rsi_slots=2, as_of?)
  Swing sleeve: partitions capital into equal slots across the two adopted
  scanners (high_base_52w breakouts x3, rsi2_pullback mean-reversion x2 —
  the validated best split). Returns per-slot ticker, quantity, rupee
  allocation, suggested stop, and forced-rotation horizon in trading days.
- momentum_scan(top_n=20)
  Top-N ranked momentum stocks under the active strategy (default
  regime_switched_momentum) with score, 52w-high proximity, returns,
  volatility, turnover.
- momentum_allocation(capital, top_n?, holdings?)
  Momentum sleeve: target portfolio (weights, quantities) and buy/sell
  orders, optionally diffed against current holdings {symbol: qty}.
- market_state()
  Current regime (BULLISH/NORMAL/CAUTION/DEFENSIVE), VIX, NIFTY 52-week
  position, stress score, target equity/gold/cash split.
- universe_lookup(symbol, on_date?)
  Point-in-time turnover rank and whether the symbol is inside the
  strategies' scan band (ranks 201-600 of the v2 momentum-grade universe).
- stock_snapshot(symbol)
  Per-ticker technical context: 1/3/6/12-month returns, 200SMA status,
  52w-high proximity, ATR(14), 20-day average turnover.

## Suggested diligence workflow

1. `market_state()` — know the regime before judging picks.
2. `swing_allocation_plan(...)` and/or `momentum_allocation(...)` — get the
   technical picks with quantities and stops.
3. Per ticker: `stock_snapshot` + `universe_lookup` for technical context,
   then YOUR research — earnings quality, promoter pledges/shareholding
   changes, pending corporate actions, regulatory news, valuation sanity.
4. Report confirm/veto per pick with reasons; never resize positions beyond
   the plan's quantities — sizing and stops are the system's domain.

## Caveats for agents

- All tools are read-only and run on vendored data (refreshed via CLI
  menu 2, "Universe update"); prices are end-of-day, not live.
- Data freshness: check the `as_of` field in every response.
- Run at most ONE process against the local DuckDB at a time (single-writer
  lock) — don't call tools while a backtest or universe update is running.
- Backtests are intentionally NOT exposed as tools (minutes-long); run them
  from the CLI (menu 6) or `fortress.actions.run_backtest` instead.
- Educational/research use only — not financial advice. See DISCLAIMER.md.
